title: According to WSJ, Nasdaq is partnering with crypto exchange Kraken to develop a framework for tokenized stock trading, enabling 24/7 trading of blockchain-ba...
snippet: Wu Blockchain (@WuBlockchain): According to WSJ, Nasdaq is partnering with crypto exchange Kraken to develop a framework for tokenized stock trading, enabling 24/7 trading of blockchain-based versions of listed equities, with the initiative expected to launch around early 2027 pending SEC approval.

title: Coinbase rolls out crypto futures trading in 26 European countries
snippet: Coinbase launched regulated crypto and equity index futures for Advanced users in 26 European countries as the ESMA sharpens scrutiny of perpetual-style products.

title: Stablecoin fintech KAST raises $80 million in Series A to fund global expansion
snippet: KAST has raised $80 million in a Series A led by QED Investors and Left Lane Capital, valuing the startup at $600 million.

title: ClearToken arrives on the Canton Network
snippet: ClearToken, the FCA-authorised digital financial market infrastructure (FMI) provider, and Canton Network today announced a strategic partnership under which ClearToken will deploy three Daml-based Digital Asset Platforms (DAPs) directly on the Canton Network: ‘CT Register’, ‘CT Pay’ and ‘CT Settle’

title: Crypto regulatory clarity matters more for banks, ex-CFTC chief says
snippet: If the CLARITY Act fails to pass, Giancarlo said he expects Paul Atkins at the SEC and Mike Selig at the CFTC will likely write rules to create clarity for the industry.

title: Stablecoin startup Kast raises $80 million
snippet: Kast, a global financial platform built on stablecoin rails founded by former Circle executive Raagulan Pathy, has raised $80 million in a Series A funding round co-led by QED Investors and Left Lane Capital.

title: Nasdaq partners with Kraken for issuer-centric tokenized equities
snippet: Nasdaq and Kraken will enable tokenized equities to move between regulated and onchain markets while preserving issuer rights and regulatory compliance.
